com.google.android.material.textfield.Ma.android:tooltipText: Choose how to authenticate with server

com.google.android.material.materialswit.android:tooltipText: Reduce bandwidth usage on slow connections
Routes remote X11 traffic to localhost:6000. Install an X server (XServer-XSDL) on this device; without one, X11 apps will fail with 'cannot open display'.

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.app:helperText: Wait this long after the last knock before opening SSH. Default: 100 ms

TextView.android:text: Status notifications stay silent. Choose if connect/disconnect events should sound or vibrate — handy for long-running tasks where a non-zero exit means something went wrong.
Auto-attach to tmux, screen, or zellij sessions

com.google.android.material.textfield.Ma.android:tooltipText: Choose how to handle multiplexer sessions on connect

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.android:tooltipText: Custom session name (e.g., main, dev). Leave empty to use default.

com.google.android.material.textfield.Ma.android:tooltipText: Override global theme for this connection

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.app:helperText: Leave empty to use global setting

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.android:tooltipText: Font size in sp (8-32). Leave empty for default.

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.app:helperText: Commands to run after connecting (one per line)

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.android:tooltipText: Shell commands to execute after SSH connection is established

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.app:helperText: One per line: KEY=value · server must allow via AcceptEnv

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.android:tooltipText: Sent via SSH SETENV. Lines beginning with # are ignored.

com.google.android.material.textfield.Te.app:helperText: Sent as the SSH exec command instead of opening a login shell. PTY allocated automatically.

TextView.android:text: For most hosts leave this at the default. Some hosts (e.g. shell.sourceforge.net) require a specific command to spawn a shell.

com.google.android.material.switchmateri.android:tooltipText: Forward your local SSH keys to the remote host (use carefully)
